DEFENDANT STIPULATES TO THE PRO TEM JUDGE. THE MATTER IS CONTINUED TO TUESDAY, JANUARY 10, 2023, AT 9:00 A.M. ON THE DISCOVERY CALENDAR BEFORE JUDGE PRO TEM RENNE. MR. VESTAL IS TO PREPARE THE PROPOSED ORDER AND IS TO SUBMIT IT TO JUDGE PRO TEM PAUL RENNE FOR REVIEW AND SIGNATURE. JUDGE PRO TEM: PAUL RENNE. CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA. MATTER NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON AUG-23-2023 IN 206 -- NO HEARING HELD -- THE COURT INFORMED THE MOVING PARTY/PLAINTIFF ON 8/22/23 THAT PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL IS GRANTED. THE TRIAL DATE OF 9/11/23 IS CONTINUED TO 2/5/24 IN DEPT. 206 AT 9:30 AM. DISCOVERY IS CLOSED IN THIS MATTER EXCEPT FOR OUTSTANDING DISCOVERY THAT WAS EITHER PENDING (E.G., PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION) OR THE SUBJECT OF MEET-AND-CONFER BETWEEN THE PARTIES AS OF JULY 5, 2023. THE CONTINUED TRIAL DATE WILL SERVE AS THE INITIAL TRIAL DATE FOR PURPOSES OF DETERMINING THE LAST DAY TO COMPLETE SAID DISCOVERY AND TO HEAR MOTIONS CONCERNING SAME, AS REFLECTED ON THE STIPULATION OF PARTIES FILED AND THE SIGNED ORDER. MOVING PARTY WAS TO CONTACT ALL OTHER PARTIES REGARDING THE GRANTING OF THE MOTION AND THAT THERE IS NO HEARING NECESSARY. (206)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plainitff'S Requests For Production Of Documents No. 15
Off Calendar Dec-13-2022 Continued To Jan-10-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Peter L. Vestal, Esq. of Niesar & Vestal LLP, Tel. 415.882.5300, for Defendant Terra Global Capital, LLC;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF NICOLE ROSSELL'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plainitff's Requests For Production Of Documents No. 15.

Defendant stipulates to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for defendant (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their own behalf.

9:19 a.m. The matter is called. The Court stated that it did not enter a tentative ruling and had ordered the parties to appear. There being no appearance by the Plaintiff, the matter is passed.

9:36 a.m. Second call of the matter. No appearance by Plaintiff.

Brief discussions are held. At the conclusion of discussions, the Court orders the parties to meet and confer to try to reach a proposed solution regarding the recreation of the documents. The Court stated that it is reserving ruling on who will bear costs on any reproduction.

The matter is continued to Tuesday, January 10, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. on the Discovery calendar before Judge Pro Tem Renne.

Mr. Vestal is to prepare the proposed order and is to submit it to Judge Pro Tem Paul Renne for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Matter not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-09-2022 Set For Trial On Feb-21-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-17-2022 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS TERRA GLOBAL CAPITAL, LLC, AND LESLIE DURSCHINGER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' demurrer to the complaint's second and third causes of action is sustained without leave to amend. California's three-year fraud statute of limitations is triggered whenever "the plaintiff suspects or should suspect that her injury was caused by wrongdoing." (CCP 338(d); Jolly v. Eli Lilly & Co. (1988) 44 Cal.3d 1103, 1110; Vera v. REL-BC, LLC (2021) 66 Cal.App.5th 57, 68-69.) Here, plaintiff pleads in detail that defendants caused her wrong on "multiple occasions" starting in December 2015 by failing to pay her while offering various excuses. (Cmplt. 2:12-3:10.) This case may continue as a breach of contract action, which is what it is. The demurrer's other grounds need not be reached.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
